post.summary: Track UTM parameters for logged-in user
post.description: Creates a new UTM tracking record when the client accesses the app via QR code, deep link, event, or referral. Each client can have multiple tracking records to capture their complete journey. Core UTM parameters are stored in dedicated columns for easy querying, while all parameters (including custom Branch.io params) are also stored as raw JSON.

requestBody.description: UTM parameters to track. The tracking_context field specifies how/where the user accessed the app.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid or empty UTM data

items.example: The utm parameters field is required.
The selected tracking context is invalid.

get.description:
Get list of finished events for a logged-in client

This method retrieves a paginated list of events that the client has finished. It involves transforming each event's data to include its taken capacity, remaining capacity, distance from the client (calculated using Google Maps), and the first photo associated with the event. The list is then returned as a collection of EventResource objects.

Get list of upcoming events for a logged-in client

Similar to the finished events list, this method fetches a paginated list of all upcoming events associated with the client. Each event is enhanced with additional data such as taken capacity, remaining capacity, distance to the event (using Google Maps), and the event's first photo. The events are then presented as a collection of EventResource objects.

post.description:
Store a new UTM entry in the database.

The `store` method accepts a POST request containing UTM parameters.
It validates the incoming data and then creates a new UTM entry in the database.
Upon successful creation, it returns a JSON response with a success message.

utm_parameters.description: UTM and Branch tracking parameters. All parameters are optional and flexible - backend will accept any keys provided. Core parameters (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_content, utm_term, promo_code, ref_code, country_code, utm_test, utm_type) will be stored in dedicated columns, while all parameters (including any future ones from Branch) will be stored in raw JSON format.

utm_type.description: UTM type - whether it came from a QR code or link

tracking_context.description: Context in which the UTM parameters were captured. Defaults to 'other' if not provided.
